title: Teaser, Thumbnail and End-Card Content Package for a Luxury Manhattan Real-Estate YouTube Channel
grounding_note: Verbatim from the source brief: a top-producing Manhattan luxury real-estate broker's YouTube channel (3-4 videos/month) needs a per-video content package of (1) a 30-90s podcast-cold-open-style TEASER with a hook in the first 5s, hard-cut before resolution, formatted for YouTube and cross-postable to LinkedIn, prestige tone, no cheap transitions; (2) a custom high-contrast click-worthy THUMBNAIL that is on-brand for luxury real estate (not a generic headshot with a text box); (3) a 10-15s branded END CARD with subscribe prompt, suggested-video and CTA, consistent across videos. Luxury-brand restraint and 48-72h turnaround are also verbatim. Reasonably enriched to make it a runnable single-video benchmark: I fixed the deliverable to ONE representative video (a 12-min Tribeca penthouse walkthrough + market-insight intro) so the chain is concrete; I named the specific source assets the client uploads (broker portrait, penthouse hero still, raw 1080p walkthrough footage with the broker's voice-over, a pre-shot branded end-card backplate, and the broker's wordmark logo PNG); and I pinned exact deliverable specs (1280x720 thumbnail base plate, 1920x1080 YouTube teaser + 1080x1080 LinkedIn teaser, 1920x1080 end card, SVG logo). What is NOT in the brief but added for benchmark runnability: the exact pixel targets, the choice of Tribeca penthouse, and the decision to treat the on-thumbnail headline TEXT as the single unavoidable local packaging step (the connector cannot set type), delivering instead a fully retouched, graded, correctly-sized, canvas-expanded thumbnail BASE plate plus the vectorized logo. No deliverable was invented that the brief did not ask for.
one_line_ask: Produce the full per-video content package for one luxury Manhattan real-estate YouTube video — a retouched high-contrast thumbnail base plate, a podcast-style teaser cut (YouTube + LinkedIn sizes) with cleaned voice-over, and a branded end card — all from the broker's supplied portrait, penthouse still, raw walkthrough footage, end-card backplate, and wordmark logo.
full_brief:
A top-producing Manhattan luxury real-estate broker runs a YouTube channel (market insights, property walkthroughs, deal-level expertise) at 3-4 videos/month and needs the full content package built around each video, with luxury-brand restraint and a 48-72h turnaround. For THIS video — a 12-minute Tribeca penthouse walkthrough that opens with a market-insight monologue — deliver three things.

THUMBNAIL: a high-contrast, click-worthy, on-brand thumbnail base plate at 1280x720, NOT a generic headshot with a text box. Start from the broker's supplied studio portrait and the penthouse hero still. Straighten and auto-tone the portrait, isolate the broker from the background so the figure reads cleanly, and grade the penthouse hero into a moody high-contrast luxury look (deepened shadows, controlled highlights, warm prestige temperature, punchy local contrast). Crop/resize the hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame and, since the wide-angle source still has room to spare, crop the graded hero to the exact 1280x720 thumbnail bounds so it fills the full frame without stretching. Also vectorize the broker's wordmark logo to a clean SVG for crisp placement at any size. The on-thumbnail HEADLINE TEXT is the single step the image connector cannot do (it cannot set type), so it is finished in one local packaging step over the delivered base plate.

TEASER: from the raw 1080p walkthrough footage (which carries the broker's lavalier voice-over), first summarize the footage to locate the single most compelling 60-90 seconds for a podcast-cold-open hook; produce an AI highlight cut to that target duration that hooks in the first 5 seconds and hard-cuts before the resolution; reframe that cut to a 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting (same length, no trim); and clean/denoise the broker's voice-over so the spoken track is broadcast-clean. Deliver the YouTube-native teaser and the LinkedIn square, with prestige tone and no cheap transitions.

END CARD: from the client's pre-shot branded end-card backplate footage (10-15s), reframe it to the exact 1920x1080 YouTube delivery dimensions so the subscribe prompt, suggested-video slot and CTA sit correctly and stay consistent across videos.

All image work must be done on the connector (straighten, tone, mask, grade, crop, vectorize); all video/audio work via the async media tools (summarize, quick-cut, resize, speech-clean). Only the final headline typesetting on the thumbnail base plate is local.

inputs.name: broker_portrait.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio headshot of a confident 45-year-old male luxury real-estate broker, charcoal tailored suit, no tie, arms loosely crossed, three-quarter turn toward camera, neutral seamless dark-gray studio backdrop, soft key light with subtle rim light, real skin pores and a few flyaway hairs, slight five-o'clock shadow, candid editorial expression, shot on 85mm f1.8, shallow depth of field, never CGI-glossy, full frame portrait orientation, sharp focus on eyes
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real photographer's studio capture: visible pores, individual hair strands, natural skin specular highlights, a clean but real seamless backdrop so the connector's subject-isolation has a believable edge to find. No retouching baked in (the connector does the retouch).

inputs.name: penthouse_hero.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal interior real-estate hero shot of a Tribeca penthouse great room at blue hour: floor-to-ceiling windows with Manhattan skyline, herringbone oak floors, low Italian sectional, marble fireplace, warm practical lamps glowing, wide-angle architectural photography, natural slightly cool ambient light mixed with warm interior lamps, real reflections and minor lens vignette, ultra-detailed, not over-saturated, shot on 16-35mm, straight verticals
inputs.realism_notes: Slightly flat/under-graded straight-out-of-camera look so the connector's grade chain (shadows, highlights, temperature, contrast, vibrance) has real headroom to work. Mixed cool-ambient/warm-lamp lighting gives the temperature and HSL steps something genuine to correct.

inputs.name: walkthrough_raw_1080p.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p handheld-stabilized real-estate walkthrough of a Tribeca penthouse: opens on the broker speaking to camera in the great room delivering a market-insight line, then a slow push through floor-to-ceiling windows revealing the skyline, then the kitchen and primary suite; natural broker lavalier voice-over describing the property and a recent comparable sale; documentary cinematography, prestige tone, native audio, 75 seconds
inputs.realism_notes: Must carry a real continuous spoken voice-over track (for media_summarize to transcribe, quick_cut to find the hook, and media_enhance_speech to denoise). Native 1080p with room tone and slight handheld motion so the reframe/resize steps have a real frame to work on.

inputs.name: endcard_backplate_1080p.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 12-second branded outro backplate for a luxury real-estate channel: slow drifting aerial of the Manhattan skyline at dusk with soft cinematic grain, lower-third clear space reserved for a subscribe button and a suggested-video card, restrained elegant motion, muted prestige color palette, native ambient music bed, 1080p
inputs.realism_notes: Shot slightly off the final 1920x1080 delivery frame (e.g. native 2048x1152 or a 9:16-ish capture) so the video_resize reframe step has a genuine dimension change to perform; clear negative space where the CTA and suggested-video card will sit.

inputs.name: broker_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Minimal luxury real-estate wordmark logo on transparent background, the text 'STERLING ROW' in an elegant high-contrast serif with refined letter-spacing, thin underline rule, monochrome charcoal, crisp clean edges, vector-ready, no gradients, no photographic texture, centered, generous margins
inputs.realism_notes: Flat high-contrast monochrome wordmark with clean edges so image_vectorize produces a faithful SVG; transparent PNG so it composites later without a matte.

outputs.name: thumbnail_base_1280x720.png
outputs.spec: 1280x720 (16:9) high-contrast luxury thumbnail BASE plate: straightened + auto-toned broker figure isolated from background, moody graded penthouse hero, cropped to the exact 1280x720 bounds from the wide-angle source (no pixels invented), no stretching. PNG, sRGB. Headline text added in the single local packaging step.

outputs.name: broker_portrait_isolated.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background PNG of the retouched broker (auto-straightened, auto-toned, subject-isolated) for flexible placement on the thumbnail and future videos.

outputs.name: logo_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the 'STERLING ROW' wordmark, scalable for thumbnail and end-card placement.

outputs.name: teaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4
outputs.spec: 30-90s podcast-cold-open teaser, hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution, 1920x1080 YouTube-native, cleaned voice-over, prestige tone, no transitions.

outputs.name: teaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4
outputs.spec: Same teaser reframed to 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting, same length (no trim), cleaned voice-over.

outputs.name: endcard_1920x1080.mp4
outputs.spec: 10-15s branded end card reframed to exactly 1920x1080 with clear space for subscribe prompt, suggested-video card and CTA, consistent across videos.

connector_workflow.note: Client uploads the five supplied assets (broker_portrait.jpg, penthouse_hero.jpg, walkthrough_raw_1080p.mp4, endcard_backplate_1080p.mp4, broker_wordmark_logo.png) into CC storage; init+finalize register them and return asset IDs/presigned URLs for the chain. Initial asset load.
Level the broker portrait so the isolated figure sits true on the thumbnail. Consumes the uploaded broker_portrait.
Balance exposure/contrast/white-balance on the straightened portrait so the broker reads cleanly before isolation. Consumes step 2.
Auto-select the broker figure on the toned portrait, producing a maskURI. Consumes step 3.
Use the subject mask to strip the studio backdrop, yielding the transparent-PNG isolated broker (a delivered output and the thumbnail's figure layer). Consumes steps 3+4.
Deepen the shadows of the penthouse hero still for the moody luxury base. Consumes the uploaded penthouse_hero (hero branch).
Recover/control the blown window highlights on the shadow-deepened hero. Consumes step hero_shadows.
Warm the mixed cool-ambient/warm-lamp hero toward a prestige temperature. Consumes step hero_highlights.
Add punchy high-contrast pop for thumbnail click-through while keeping luxury restraint. Consumes step hero_warmed.
Final vibrance lift on the graded hero (protect skin/wood tones, lift skyline). Completes the moody luxury grade. Consumes step hero_punchy.
Crop/resize the graded hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame toward 1280x720. Consumes step hero_graded.

Crop the 16:9 hero to the exact 1280x720 thumbnail bounds so it fills the full frame with no stretching (gen-free reframe: the wide-angle source has room to spare, so no pixels are invented). Produces the delivered thumbnail BASE plate. Consumes step 11.
Vectorize the supplied STERLING ROW wordmark PNG to a clean scalable SVG for thumbnail and end-card placement. Consumes the uploaded broker_wordmark_logo.
Summarize/transcribe the raw walkthrough footage to locate the single most compelling 60-90s window for the podcast-cold-open hook. Consumes the uploaded walkthrough_raw_1080p. Async-widget.

AI highlight cut of the walkthrough to the hook target duration (hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution), guided by the summary, delivered 1920x1080 for YouTube. Consumes the footage + step 14. Async-widget.
Reframe the YouTube teaser to a 1080x1080 LinkedIn square, same length, no trim. Consumes step 15. Async-widget.

Clean/denoise the broker's lavalier voice-over on the teaser cut so the spoken track is broadcast-clean (voice only). Consumes step 15. Async-widget; the cleaned VO is muxed back over the teaser deliverables.

Reframe the supplied branded end-card backplate to exactly 1920x1080 (same length, no trim) so the subscribe/suggested-video/CTA zones sit correctly. Consumes the uploaded endcard_backplate. Async-widget.

chaining_note: Everything flows from one initial asset load (step 1, asset_initialize_file_upload), which registers the five client-supplied assets and is the only step with no inputs_from. THREE chains branch off it and every post-load step consumes a prior step's named output. THUMBNAIL figure chain: uploaded portrait -> auto_straighten(portrait_straightened) -> apply_auto_tone(portrait_toned) -> select_subject(portrait_subject_mask) -> remove_background consumes BOTH portrait_toned + portrait_subject_mask -> broker_portrait_isolated (delivered). THUMBNAIL hero chain: uploaded hero -> adjust_dark_portions(hero_shadows) -> adjust_highlights(hero_highlights) -> adjust_color_temperature(hero_warmed) -> adjust_brightness_and_contrast(hero_punchy) -> adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(hero_graded) -> crop_and_resize(hero_16x9) -> crop_to_bounds(thumbnail_base_1280x720, delivered). The final step is a gen-free reframe: the wide-angle source has room to spare, so the graded 16:9 hero is cropped down to the exact 1280x720 bounds and no pixels are invented. LOGO: uploaded wordmark -> vectorize(logo_wordmark, delivered SVG). VIDEO chain: uploaded walkthrough -> media_summarize(walkthrough_summary) -> video_create_quick_cut consumes footage + walkthrough_summary -> teaser_youtube_1920x1080 (delivered); that teaser then feeds video_resize -> teaser_linkedin_1080x1080 (delivered) AND media_enhance_speech -> teaser_vo_cleaned (muxed into both teaser deliverables). END CARD: uploaded backplate -> video_resize -> endcard_1920x1080 (delivered). Each of the six deliverables is the terminal node of a chain; no step is orphaned. The one local packaging step (typesetting the headline onto thumbnail_base_1280x720) is explicitly outside the connector workflow because the image connector cannot set type.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and genuinely wide-mix: 17 connector calls spanning 17 distinct Adobe tools across three connector groups (image_* raster, asset_* storage, and the async video/audio media_* family), well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar and into flagship territory (>=12 calls, >=3 groups, deep output->input chaining). Difficulty comes from (a) a 6-step luxury color-grade chain that must be ordered correctly (shadows -> highlights -> temperature -> contrast -> vibrance) before crop and reframe-crop; (b) a 4-step retouch-then-isolate sub-chain where remove_background must consume both the toned image AND the subject mask; (c) honest execution-mode discipline — every image step is headless [C], but the entire teaser/end-card track is async-widget [A], so the agent must know which deliverables are product-interactive vs autonomous; and (d) respecting the hard not-available boundaries: no gen-fill (only outpaint via generative-expand), no compositing, and acknowledging that the on-thumbnail headline TYPE is the single step the connector cannot do, so the connector delivers a retouched/graded/sized/expanded base plate plus a vectorized logo rather than a finished captioned image. A weaker agent will misorder the grade, try to typeset or composite text headlessly, attempt a forbidden gen-fill or bg-replace-by-prompt, or assume the [A] video steps return retrievable files in a headless run.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains anywhere in the spec after the fix. The workflow array's only reframe to final thumbnail bounds is image_crop_to_bounds (headless [C]) — a crop of the already-16:9 graded hero down to exactly 1280x720, inventing no pixels. All stale generative language is now reconciled: chaining_note no longer names generative-expand/reframe-crop, and outputs[0].spec no longer says 'canvas outpainted' (now 'cropped to the exact 1280x720 bounds, no pixels invented'). No upscale, text-to-image, from-scratch art, object-removal-with-reconstruction, or compositing (isolated broker and base plate delivered as separate layers; headline TYPE punted to one local packaging step). image_remove_background yields a transparent PNG via the subject mask (not bg-replace-by-prompt). Both video_resize calls reframe to new dimensions at same length (no trim). All 17 distinct tools are server=adobe, real connector actions in [C]/[A] modes.
reverify.notes: Fixes were documentation-only: category corrected from headshot_portrait to multi_deliverable_content_package; chaining_note and outputs[0].spec reconciled to the gen-free crop method already present in the workflow array. Executable workflow, inputs, tools_used, tool_call_count(18), and distinct_adobe_tools(17) are unchanged and internally consistent. No IP/naming changes required — STERLING ROW is an invented fictional wordmark, and all depicted subjects are generic (unnamed broker).
